2708.reason: [Error: The line number for the missing semicolon error should be the line of the last non-terminal before the semicolon, not semicolonExp.getLine() - 1. This causes incorrect error line reporting.]

2.reason: [The program assumes the input file exists and is a valid source program; if the file is missing or the environment (e.g., javac) fails to compile the Java code itself, the program may never run, resulting in empty output. In the given test, the shell could not find javac, so the Java program was never executed.]

2705.reason: [This line incorrectly consumes a token that may not be a semicolon. When BlockItemParser fails to properly handle a VarDecl and passes control to StmtParser, this code path reads the next token (which could be printf) as if it were a semicolon, creating an erroneous semicolon token and causing the extra SEMICN output in the buggy case.]
